steps.title: Set up the subtraction
steps.content:
Substitute the expressions for $a$ and $b$ into $a - b$ and distribute the negative sign carefully:
$$a - b = (3k + 5r) - (9k - 12r + 6)$$
$$= 3k + 5r - 9k + 12r - 6$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Group and combine:
$k$ terms: $3k - 9k = -6k$
$r$ terms: $5r + 12r = 17r$
constant: $-6$
So $a - b = -6k + 17r - 6$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $-6k + 17r - 6$: Matches our simplified expression exactly.
  • B) $-6k + 17r + 6$: Wrong sign on the constant; subtracting $+6$ gives $-6$, not $+6$.
  • C) $-6k - 7r + 6$: Wrong $r$ coefficient ($5r - (-12r) = 17r$, not $-7r$) and wrong constant.
  • D) $-6k - 7r - 6$: Wrong $r$ coefficient; sign of $-12r$ must flip to $+12r$.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
